title: 10 best herbal teas for upset stomach
intro_desc: Herbal teas for upset stomach are a type of remedy that helps alleviate digestive discomfort by soothing the stomach and intestines. They contain natural ingredients that have anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which can ease nausea, bloating, and cramps associated with upset stomach. The best herbal teas for this issue include Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Matricaria chamomilla, Taraxacum officinale, Mentha x piperita, Peumus boldus, Aloe barbadensis, Ginkgo biloba, and Cinchona officinalis. If left untreated, an upset stomach can lead to missed work or school, social isolation, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting one's overall quality of life and daily activities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is good for upset stomach because it helps to calm down the digestive system. When we eat something that doesn't agree with us, our stomach can get upset, causing nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain. Ginger tea works by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which helps to ease the discomfort. It also helps to move food through the digestive system faster, which can prevent the stomach from getting too full and causing pain. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. As a result, ginger tea can provide quick relief from stomach upset, making it a popular remedy for nausea and digestive issues.

Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a natural remedy for upset stomach. The tea made from its seeds has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. This is because fennel seeds contain compounds that help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps and spasms. Fennel tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract. Additionally, it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting. The anise-like flavor of fennel tea can also help to stimulate digestion and relieve bloating. When consumed as a tea, fennel seeds can help to ease symptoms of indigestion, gas, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, can help ease an upset stomach. The menthol in peppermint tea helps relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol also helps to slow down digestion and reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as bloating and gas. Additionally, peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation that can cause stomach pain and nausea. This makes peppermint tea a popular remedy for stomach issues, including nausea, indigestion, and stomach cramps. Drinking peppermint tea can also help to calm the digestive system, making it easier to digest food and reducing the risk of stomach upset after eating.

Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is often used to soothe an upset stomach. This is because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When we eat something bad or get a stomach bug, it can cause inflammation in the stomach lining, leading to pain and discomfort. Glycyrrhizin helps to reduce this inflammation, making it easier for the stomach to heal. Additionally, licorice root tea has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to slow down digestion and give the stomach a break. This can help to prevent further irritation and allow the stomach to recover.

Cinchona officinalis tea is a good remedy for an upset stomach. The main reason is that it contains quinine, a natural substance that helps calm down the stomach muscles. When you have an upset stomach, these muscles can get too active, causing nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Quinine in the tea helps to slow down these muscles, so they don't work too hard. This makes it easier to digest food and reduces the discomfort in the stomach. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive system. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis tea has been used for centuries to treat fever and other stomach problems, making it a trusted natural remedy for many people. Its soothing properties can help ease stomach cramps and spasms, providing relief from the pain and discomfort of an upset stomach. The tea's natural antiseptic properties also help to prevent infections, which can further irritate the stomach and make symptoms worse.

Peumus boldus tea is a natural remedy for upset stomach. It is made from the leaves of the boldo tree, which is native to South America. The tea has been used for centuries to soothe digestive problems. The main reason it is good for an upset stomach is that it helps to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation. It contains compounds that work as an anti-inflammatory, which helps to ease the burning and discomfort in the stomach. This makes it easier to digest food and reduces the risk of nausea and vomiting. The tea also helps to regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation or diarrhea. By reducing inflammation and regulating digestion, Peumus boldus tea can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with an upset stomach.

Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, is a good remedy for upset stomach. This tea is made from the leaves of the nettle plant, which have been used for centuries to soothe digestive problems. The main reason it's helpful is that it contains compounds called flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink nettle tea, these compounds help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can ease symptoms like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Additionally, nettle tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing. This can be especially helpful for people who experience stomach problems due to stress, anxiety, or food sensitivities. Drinking nettle tea regularly may also help regulate bowel movements and improve nutrient absorption, leading to a healthier digestive system.

Taraxacum officinale tea, also known as dandelion tea, is a popular remedy for an upset stomach. The roots and leaves of the dandelion plant have been used for centuries to treat digestive issues. The tea is made by steeping the roots and leaves in hot water, releasing their healing properties. It's said to work by soothing the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. The tea is also a natural diuretic, which means it helps the body get rid of excess fluids and salts that can cause bloating and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as potassium, which can help replace lost electrolytes and balance the body's fluids. When consumed, the tea is believed to ease symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and stomach cramps, providing relief from an upset stomach.

Aloe barbadensis tea is a natural remedy for an upset stomach. The main reason is its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract. This soothes the stomach lining and prevents further damage. The tea also contains compounds that slow down the movement of food through the digestive system, allowing the stomach to recover. It has a soothing effect on the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can help calm nausea and cramps. Additionally, Aloe barbadensis tea has antibacterial properties that help combat infections in the digestive system. This can help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach, such as bloating, gas, and stomach pain.

Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is a natural remedy for upset stomach. The tea is made from the flowers of the lavender plant, which contain compounds that help to soothe and calm the digestive system. When you drink the tea, the compounds travel through your body and reach your stomach, where they help to reduce inflammation and ease cramps. This can be especially helpful if you have irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or other digestive issues that cause stomach pain and discomfort. The tea also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate stomach problems. Additionally, lavender contains antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infections and promote healing in the digestive tract. As a result, drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea may help to alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach, such as bloating, nausea, and stomach cramps.
